source.sourcePath: raw/sources/RA/01_DHF/05_HnX-P1, HnX-PB/03_설계개발 출력/10_User manual/[HnX-PB] User manual(US) (A-IFU-HNX(B)-US Rev 0.3, 260312).docx

sourceSummary.oneLine: User manual and service information for the HnX-PB Portable X-ray System.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Provides operating instructions, safety precautions, regulatory information, technical data, maintenance, calibration, and service information for HnX-PB users and service personnel.
keyPoints: The HnX-PB is a battery-powered portable X-ray System designed and manufactured by H&abyz.
The portable x-ray system may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities and is not for mammography use.
The manual states that portable diagnostic X-ray equipment shall only be used when it is medically inadvisable to move a patient to a fixed radiographic or fixed fluoroscopic installation.
The HnX-PB conforms to IEC 60601-1-2:2020 for both immunity and emissions.

Software and firmware updates cannot be performed directly by users in the field and must be performed by authorized service personnel or by returning the product to the manufacturer or service center.

entities.evidence: H&abyz A1417MCW / A1717MCW / F1417MCW declared in K223930

entities.name: Food and Drug Administration

concepts.name: Indication for use
concepts.evidence: The portable x-ray system may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities.

concepts.name: Restricted portable diagnostic X-ray use
concepts.evidence: This equipment shall only be used when it is medically inadvisable to move a patient to a fixed radiographic or fixed fluoroscopic installation.

concepts.name: Radiation safety
concepts.evidence: When X-ray is being irradiated, the operator shall be stand at least 1.8meters(6feet) from the patient, the X-ray tube, and out of the useful beam.

concepts.name: Facility access controls
concepts.evidence: This device requires facility-implemented access controls.

concepts.name: Authorized operators
concepts.evidence: Facilities must maintain a current written list of all authorized operators including: Full name, license number and expiration date, training completion date etc.

concepts.name: Mandatory usage log
concepts.evidence: MANDATORY USAGE LOG: Document every device use with: Date and time (start/end), Operator name and license number, Patient identifier (if applicable), Examination type and anatomical area, Any incidents or malfunctions

concepts.name: Cybersecurity response
concepts.evidence: If a cybersecurity threat is detected, please disconnect the Bluetooth connection first, turn off the device, and then contact the H&abyz service team.

concepts.name: Software and firmware update restriction
concepts.evidence: the product's software and firmware updates cannot be performed directly by users in the field, and must be performed by authorized service personnel or by returning the product to the manufacturer or service center.

concepts.name: EMC safety
concepts.evidence: HnX-PB conforms to this IEC60601-1-2:2020 standard for both immunity and emissions.

concepts.name: Essential performance
concepts.evidence: The error in the indicated value of the X-ray tube voltage, in any combination of loading factors, shall be not greater than 10%.

concepts.name: Annual preventive inspection and maintenance
concepts.evidence: The following inspection and maintenance for the unit shall be performed once every year, and handled by a service person only

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is a portable X-ray System.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is a portable X-ray System.

evidence.claim: The user manual only pertains to the HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: This User Manual only pertains to the HnX-PB and does not serve for any other products of the company.

evidence.claim: H&abyz Co., Ltd. is identified as the manufacturer.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturer: H&abyz Co., Ltd.

evidence.claim: The product is a medical device.
evidence.evidenceText: This product is a medical device

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is made in Korea.
evidence.evidenceText: Manufacturing country: Made in Korea

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is battery-powered.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is a battery-powered portable X-ray System, designed and manufactured by H&abyz.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities.
evidence.evidenceText: The portable x-ray system may be used for diagnostic imaging of body extremities.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is not intended for mammography.
evidence.evidenceText: This HnX-PB is not intended for mammography.

evidence.claim: The device is not intended to replace a stationary radiographic system.
evidence.evidenceText: This device is not intended to replace a stationary radiographic system, which may be required for full optimization of image quality and radiation exposure for different exam types.

evidence.claim: Portable diagnostic X-ray equipment is restricted to use when moving a patient to a fixed installation is medically inadvisable.
evidence.evidenceText: This equipment shall only be used when it is medically inadvisable to move a patient to a fixed radiographic or fixed fluoroscopic installation.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB is not intended for fluoroscopic use.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is designed for radiographic uses only and not intended for fluoroscopic use or other associated applications.

evidence.claim: The device does not apply to dental radiography.
evidence.evidenceText: The device does not apply to mammography and dental radiography.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB should be operated only by trained and qualified professionals.
evidence.evidenceText: This product should be operated only by or under the supervision of a properly trained and qualified professional.

evidence.claim: Facilities must maintain a written list of authorized operators.
evidence.evidenceText: Facilities must maintain a current written list of all authorized operators including: Full name, license number and expiration date, training completion date etc.

evidence.claim: Every device use must be documented in a usage log.
evidence.evidenceText: MANDATORY USAGE LOG: Document every device use with: Date and time (start/end), Operator name and license number, Patient identifier (if applicable), Examination type and anatomical area, Any incidents or malfunctions

evidence.claim: When not in active use, the device must be stored in a locked or secured location or under direct supervision.
evidence.evidenceText: REQUIRED STORAGE: When not in active use, store device in: Locked equipment room with access limited to authorized personnel, OR Secured with physical locking mechanism (cable lock, equipment cage), OR Under direct supervision of authorized staff.

evidence.claim: If unauthorized use is suspected, the device should be powered off and secured, and the supervisor and radiation safety officer notified within 2 hours.
evidence.evidenceText: IMMEDIATE RESPONSE: If unauthorized use suspected: Power off device immediately, Secure device location, Document incident details, Notify supervisor and radiation safety officer within 2 hours

evidence.claim: The operator must stand at least 1.8 meters from the patient and X-ray tube and out of the useful beam during irradiation.
evidence.evidenceText: When X-ray is being irradiated, the operator shall be stand at least 1.8meters(6feet) from the patient, the X-ray tube, and out of the useful beam.

evidence.claim: During X-ray exposure, operators should wear protective clothing of at least 0.5 mm lead equivalence or 1.6 mm lead equivalence and stand behind shielding.
evidence.evidenceText: During X-ray exposure, the operator should wear a qualified protective clothing e.g. apron of min. 0.5 mm lead equivalence or 1.6mm(1/16inch) lead equivalence and stand behind the lead screen or the lead glass screen

evidence.claim: The device is Class I and IPX0 equipment under EN 60601-1 standards.
evidence.evidenceText: The HnX-PB is specified as Class I and IPX0 equipment under EN 60601-1 standards.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB conforms to IEC 60601-1-2:2020 for immunity and emissions.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX-PB conforms to this IEC60601-1-2:2020 standard for both immunity and emissions.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB requires special EMC precautions and must be put into service according to EMC information in the user manual.
evidence.evidenceText: HnX-PB requires special precautions regarding EMC and needs to be operated only by authorized personnel and put into service according to EMC information provided in the user manual.

evidence.claim: Users must not use devices that generate RF signals near the HnX-PB.
evidence.evidenceText: Do not use any device for generating RF signals near the device, otherwise it may lead to malfunction in the device, or even unexpected results.

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB software/firmware is HnX_P1 version 00.12.
evidence.evidenceText: Software(Firmware): HnX_P1 Version: 00.12

evidence.claim: Software and firmware updates cannot be performed directly by users in the field.
evidence.evidenceText: the product's software and firmware updates cannot be performed directly by users in the field, and must be performed by authorized service personnel or by returning the product to the manufacturer or service center.

evidence.claim: If a cybersecurity threat is detected, users should disconnect Bluetooth, turn off the device, and contact the H&abyz service team.
evidence.evidenceText: If a cybersecurity threat is detected, please disconnect the Bluetooth connection first, turn off the device, and then contact the H&abyz service team.

evidence.claim: The cybersecurity risk of the HnX-PB was addressed as part of device risk analysis.
evidence.evidenceText: The cybersecurity risk of the HnX-PB have been addressed to assure that no new or increased cybersecurity risks were introduced as a part of device risk analysis.

evidence.claim: The maximum output of the X-ray generator is 720 W.
evidence.evidenceText: The maximum output of X-ray generator is 720W

evidence.claim: The X-ray tube output voltage range is 50 to 90 kV.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray Tube output voltage of generator is 50~90kV

evidence.claim: The X-ray tube focal spot is 0.5 mm by 0.5 mm.
evidence.evidenceText: The X-ray Tube focal spot is 0.5㎜ x 0.5㎜

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB has total filtration of 3.0 mm Al.
evidence.evidenceText: Total Filtration : 3.0mm Al

evidence.claim: The HnX-PB image acquisition detector options include H&abyz A1417MCW, A1717MCW, and F1417MCW declared in K223930, or alternatives cleared by U.S. 510(k).
evidence.evidenceText: Image Acquisition H&abyz A1417MCW, A1717MCW, F1417MCW : declared in K223930 or alternatives U.S. 510(k) cleared

evidence.claim: The device uses IEC 60601-1, IEC 60601-1-2, IEC 60601-1-3, IEC 60601-2-28, IEC 60601-2-54, IEC 62304, IEC 62366-1, IEC 60601-1-6, ISO 15223-1, radio EMC, ETSI, FCC, and 21 CFR standards or regulatory references.
evidence.evidenceText: Product safety standards IEC 60601-1: 2005/AMD2:2020 ... FCC SUB PART C ... 21 CFR 1020 Subchapter J: Performance Standards for Ionizing Radiation Emitting Products ... 21 CFR 1020.30: Diagnostic x-ray system and their major components

evidence.claim: Annual preventive inspection and maintenance must be performed by a service person only.
evidence.evidenceText: The following inspection and maintenance for the unit shall be performed once every year, and handled by a service person only

evidence.claim: The estimated product lifetime is up to 7 years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.
evidence.evidenceText: The estimated product lifetime is up to 7 years under appropriate regular inspection and maintenance.

evidence.claim: Main repair parts will be stocked for 5 years after discontinuance of production.
evidence.evidenceText: Main parts (parts required to maintain the function of the product) of this product will be stocked for 5 years after discontinuance of production for repairing.

reviewFlags.reason: Document revision and date were extracted from the source filename because the body text does not explicitly state a manual revision or document date.
The date string 260312 appears in the filename but is not explicitly defined in the source text as a document date.
Authority signal is general, while the text references FDA guidance, U.S. 510(k), FCC, and 21 CFR without naming a single issuing authority for the manual.
Some figure and symbol content appears to be lost or flattened in preprocessing, including icon references and label figures.
The source includes inconsistent terminology and apparent OCR or drafting errors, such as 'KULL' in the technique chart, 'enen', and repeated or mismatched calibration channel descriptions.
